topic: A person opening a bottle of Coca-Cola with the new attached tethered cap, side view.

parsed_generation.script:
Scene 1 — Setup (0-2s): Side view of a hand approaching a Coca-Cola bottle; the tethered cap sits at the top, clearly attached.
Scene 2 — Engage (2-5s): Side view; fingers grip bottle; tethered cap twists and lifts, still attached by the tether.
Scene 3 — Open (5-8s): Side view; cap fully rotated to open; tethered cap remains attached, bottle ready to pour.
parsed_generation.narration: The tethered cap keeps the seal nearby, reducing cap loss and clutter. The side view highlights one-handed manipulation, showcasing ease of use. Seeing the cap stay attached communicates cleanliness and controlled access in everyday packaging.

feedback: The prompt stays tightly focused on the tethered-cap Coca-Cola bottle in a side view with a clear, three-step action. To improve, tighten the narration to emphasize the tethered cap as the central feature across all scenes, and ensure the opened state clearly leads to pouring. Consider adding a brief cue about label visibility for brand clarity and reducing any extraneous motion not essential to the opening action.
strengths: Clear, sequential scene breakdown focused on the tethered cap.
One-handed interaction is consistently highlighted in the visuals.
fixes: Strongly emphasize the tethered cap as the focal point across scenes (e.g., references to tether integrity, cap remains attached).
Incorporate a explicit cue for pouring readiness to reinforce the opening outcome and user action.
